kongming.historical_notes:
Sun Qian, styled Gongyou, hailed from Beihai. When Liu Bei took command of Xuzhou Sun Qian was recommended to him by Zheng Xuan, and was appointed an aide, and followed Liu Bei across the land. When Liu Bei left Cao Cao he sent Sun Qian to establish relations with Yuan Shao, and before entering Jingzhou Sun Qian was dispatched with Mi Zhu to meet Liu Biao—and both missions went smoothly.

While in Jingzhou Liu Biao wrote Yuan Shang to express how troubled he became over his separation with Yuan Tan while discussing with Liu Bei and Sun Qian. This shows how highly he regarded Sun Qian’s view. When Liu Bei took authority in Yizhou Sun Qian was promoted from Gentleman Attendant to General Who Upholds Loyalty, the same rank as Jian Yong. Some time later he passed away. (1)

(1): Primary source: Sanguozhi translation by Lady Wu.

Sun Qian, styledGongyou, was fromBeihai[北海].
When theFormer LordledXu province[徐州], he appointed him to serve asClerk.ALater, he followed in travels and movements. When theFormer LordbetrayedExcellency Cao[荊州], he sent[Sun] Qianto ally withYuan Shao, and when he was about to go toJing province, he sent[Sun] Qianagain along withMi Zhuto both be envoys toLiu Biao, and they did all according to what was intended. Later,[Liu] Biaowrote toYuan Shang, talking about the disturbance of brothers fighting, stating:
TheBiography ofZheng Xuanstates:[Zheng] Xuanrecommended[Sun] Qianto the province.[Sun] Qianreceiving his office was thus by the recommendation of[Zheng] Xuan.
“Each time I,General of the LeftLiu, andSunGongyouhave discussed this matter together, never was there an instance where we were not pained to the bone and felt extremely sad and distressed.”
He was valued in such a manner.
When theFormer LordstabilizedYi province[益州],[Sun] Qianwent from beingGentleman Attendantto be appointed asGeneral who Maintains Loyalty. His received respects were second to that ofMi Zhu,Jian Yong, and others of the same sort. Shortly after, he died.
